place: Hamilton Square CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Hamilton Square was founded in 1692 and named after Alexander Hamilton during the anti‑British wave of the War of 1812.
A 1954 book by Helen Almy West, "A History of Hamilton Township," documents the earliest days of the municipality and is now a rare collector’s item.
In 1756 George May built a shipyard and trading post near Babcock Creek, giving the area its first commercial foothold.
The West Jersey and Atlantic Railroad reached Mays Landing in 1880, linking the town to the growing rail network of New Jersey.
The historic Mays Landing Water Power Company, founded in 1903, once employed the town’s largest workforce and still houses the Hamilton Historical Society today.
